items.description: SLICE-0108 SPIKE (PRD-0018, blockers: none). Resolve one unknown the read-only search contract depends on: when a qmd collection has been registered (qmd collection add) but never embedded, does `qmd query` against it ERROR or quietly return empty? The answer decides whether the read-only search slice's membership branch must treat present-but-unembedded as a warn-and-skip case, or only entirely-absent collections. This item ships a characterization test (not feature code) that reproduces both states against the existing qmd mock/integration surface in tests/, plus a recorded finding. Real qmd binary may be absent: if so, use the existing qmd mock/fake in the test suite (see tests/qmd-collections.test.ts and the qmd command indirection in src/integrations/qmd.ts) as the safe local substitute and characterize the mock's contract, noting in progress what remains unverified against a real binary.
steps: Inspect src/integrations/qmd.ts (updateCollection/refreshCollections/embedCollection/query) and tests/qmd-collections.test.ts to find the existing qmd command indirection and how tests stub it.

Add a characterization test in tests/qmd-collections.test.ts (or a sibling test file) that drives a query against a collection that was added but never embedded, asserting the observed behavior (error vs empty result) of the qmd surface as the suite can exercise it.

Record the finding explicitly in the SLICE-0108 file's ## Evidence section in the vault is NOT required at runtime; instead record it in .ralph/progress.md AND as a comment atop the new test, so the SLICE-0109 iteration can read it from git history.
Run all verification gates green (bun run build, bunx tsc --noEmit, bun run test) and commit; the new passing characterization test is this item's verification signal.

items.description: SLICE-0109 READ-ONLY SEARCH (PRD-0018, blockers: SLICE-0108). Make `wiki search` a pure read against whatever `wiki sync` last produced. In src/cli/verbs/search.ts remove the per-collection `qmd update` refresh from the search read path (today search calls refreshCollections(qmdCommand, collections) before querying). Collapse the N per-project ensureCollection membership probes into ONE `qmd collection list` call up front, and use that single returned set for three things: which collections to query, the add-only-missing decision, and a never-synced detector (an absent collection has never been synced -> warn to stderr suggesting `wiki sync --project <name>` and SKIP it, never a silent empty, never silent auto-register of an unembedded collection). Honor the SLICE-0108 finding: if query errors on a present-but-unembedded collection, treat that as warn-and-skip too. Reconcile the shared refreshCollections seam (src/integrations/qmd.ts, also called by src/artifacts/dedup.ts): dedup runs at create time and KEEPS its refresh; search DROPS it; assert the split by test, not by side effect. Reconcile the `--no-refresh` flag with the new default. Update the wiki skill doc to drop the 'search updates the keyword index' promise. Land atomically in one commit so the silent-empty footgun never ships between commits.
steps: Read src/cli/verbs/search.ts, src/integrations/qmd.ts (refreshCollections/updateCollection/ensureCollection), src/artifacts/dedup.ts, and tests/search-upgrade.test.ts to map the current refresh + membership flow.

Remove the per-query qmd update from search; replace the N membership probes with one `qmd collection list`; query only listed collections; warn-and-skip never-synced (and per the spike, present-but-unembedded) collections to stderr.
Keep dedup's create-time refresh intact; reconcile the --no-refresh flag with the new read-only default; update the wiki skill doc text.

Update the two pinned tests/search-upgrade.test.ts cases that assert refresh-on-search ('auto-refresh runs by default' and '--no-refresh skips updateCollection call') to the new contract; do not silently delete tests. Add a test asserting exactly one `qmd collection list` regardless of project count and zero qmd update/embed during search.
Run all verification gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0110 STRUCTURE TREE SKELETON (PRD-0019, blockers: none). Extend the per-vault method-based `Structure` (PRD-0021: buildStructure precomputes reverse maps; methods specFor/typeForId/kindForSkill/artifactTypeForVaultPath in src/artifacts/registry.ts) from a flat kind map into a one-level section/bucket tree. A section is a top-level folder owning an id prefix and id-space, declared as a BRANCH (holds buckets, no artifacts directly) XOR a LEAF (one template, holds artifacts). A bucket is a leaf inside a branch section, owning a template and a `criteria` string and sharing its section's prefix. The reverse-map precompute generalizes from per-kind to per-section/bucket. The bundled DEFAULT tree reproduces today's five kinds and six doc categories EXACTLY so an unconfigured vault is byte-for-byte unaffected. Config load validates the tree (unique bucket names, branch-XOR-leaf invariant) and hard-errors on a malformed tree, writing nothing. Walking skeleton: the type and loader carry the tree end-to-end; create/doctor/relocation behavior changes land in later slices.
steps: Read src/artifacts/registry.ts fully (Structure type, buildStructure, loadStructure, DEFAULT_STRUCTURE, DOC_CATEGORIES) to see exactly what a flat kind looks like today.
Introduce the section/bucket tree types and extend buildStructure to precompute per-section/bucket reverse maps; keep the existing Structure methods working.
Define the bundled default tree so a no-config vault produces byte-identical create/doctor/nextId behavior; the 5 kinds + 6 doc categories are modeled as ordinary sections (no privileged kinds).
Make config load validate the tree and hard-error (writing nothing) on a duplicate bucket name or a branch-and-leaf node, with a clear message.
Add tests: no-config falls back to default and existing paths are unchanged; a malformed tree fails at load. Run all gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0111 PER-SECTION ID ALLOCATION (PRD-0019, blockers: SLICE-0110). Move id allocation from per-kind to per-section so all buckets under one branch section draw from a single shared increasing id-space keyed on the section's prefix. Today src/artifacts/id.ts nextId() keys on structure.specFor(type).prefix and scans the type's directory; doc already scans recursively across categories sharing the DOC id-space. Generalize so nextId and the id-index (src/artifacts/id-index.ts / buildIdIndex) key on the SECTION. This is the mechanism that makes a later intra-section move identity-preserving. Under the default tree this preserves today's per-kind sequences exactly.
steps: Read src/artifacts/id.ts (nextId) and the id-index builder to see how allocation and the PREFIX_TO_TYPE/typeForId reverse maps key today.
Change allocation to key on the section so buckets in one section share one increasing id-space; keep the default tree's single-bucket kinds on their current sequences.
Add a test on a custom multi-bucket tree: creating into two buckets of one section yields sequential ids sharing the section prefix (one counter, not two).
Add a default-tree regression test: existing kinds keep their current id sequence. Run all gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0112 CREATE BY BUCKET/LEAF NAME (PRD-0019, blockers: SLICE-0110, SLICE-0111). Make `wiki create <name>` resolve <name> to its section (for prefix + id-space) and the bucket's body template, where <name> is a bucket inside a branch section or a leaf section. Names are unique within a project config (validated at load in SLICE-0110). An unknown name errors clearly. The artifact lands in the bucket's folder with a section-prefixed id minted per-section. This subsumes today's --category flag and doc recategorize's category argument. CRITICAL for the later deletion: the create path in src/cli/verbs/create.ts and the type in src/artifacts/store.ts must migrate off the DocCategory/isDocCategory import to the section/bucket model, leaving no dangling doc-category type for SLICE-0117. The agent picks the bucket by reading its criteria; the tool only validates the bucket exists and applies its template (no fuzzy matching).
steps: Read src/cli/verbs/create.ts and src/artifacts/store.ts (CreateInput.category: DocCategory, artifactPath usage) to map every place --category / DocCategory flows.
Resolve create by bucket/leaf name to section + template; mint a section-prefixed id; file into the bucket folder; error clearly on unknown name.
Migrate store.ts create path off DocCategory/isDocCategory to the section/bucket model so no dangling doc-category type remains for the deletion slice.
Add tests: create <bucket> files to the right folder with the right template and a section-prefixed id; unknown bucket errors; --category behavior is subsumed. Run all gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0113 DOCTOR STRUCTURE-ONLY (PRD-0019, blockers: SLICE-0110). Reframe doctor's structural validation in src/bootstrap/doctor.ts to read the config tree instead of the hardcoded category lock: every folder must be a config-declared section or bucket, and a branch section must hold no loose files directly (preserving ADR-0028's no-loose-files invariant, now expressed as config). doctor validates structural truth only (folder declared, no loose files, ids unique, frontmatter matches the bucket template) and NEVER emits a fuzzy 'wrong bucket' warning, since bucket fitness is agent judgment. This slice only READS the tree; it does NOT delete the old DOC_CATEGORIES machinery (that deletion is SLICE-0117, after all consumers migrate).
steps: Read src/bootstrap/doctor.ts to find the current locked-category folder check.
Reframe the check to validate folders against the config tree (declared section/bucket; no loose files in a branch section).
Keep the old DOC_CATEGORIES machinery importable for now (do not delete here); only change doctor's validation logic.
Add tests: an undeclared folder is flagged; a loose file in a branch section is flagged; a valid-but-debatable bucket choice is NOT flagged. Run all gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0114 GENERIC PARENT BACKLINK (PRD-0019, blockers: SLICE-0110, SLICE-0112). Replace the hardcoded PRD<->slice backlink with a config-declared relationship. A child section/bucket declares parent: <section-name>; the parent section declares which list field receives the child id via child_list: <field> (today's prd.slices). On create, resolve the child's parent, read the parent artifact, and append the new child id to that list field (no double-add, create-if-absent). This must replace BOTH hardcoded prd/slice specials in src/cli/verbs/create.ts: the pre-flight parent read AND the backlinkParentPrd write. After this the PRD<->slice link is pure config: slice declares parent: prds, prds declares child_list: slices, with no kind name hardcoded in create.
steps: Read src/cli/verbs/create.ts to find both prd/slice specials: the pre-flight parent read and backlinkParentPrd (the only place the slices backlink is written).
Add parent + child_list config fields and a generic resolve-parent/append-child-id flow with no-double-add and create-if-absent.
Replace both hardcoded specials with the generic path; keep the PRD<->slice link working purely from config.
Add tests: a child declaring parent gets its id appended to the parent's child_list on create; no double-add; create-if-absent. Run all gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0115 RELOCATION PRESERVES IDENTITY (PRD-0019, blockers: SLICE-0111, SLICE-0112). Generalize relocateArtifact / doc recategorize (src/artifacts/store.ts, src/cli/verbs/doc.ts) into a section-agnostic 'move to bucket'. Same-section move keeps the artifact id (inbound [[id]] links stay resolvable) because the section owns the id-space. Cross-section move RE-MINTS the id (the settled rule; cross-section moves are rare; no link-rewriting in this PRD). This is largely generalizing what half-exists: nextId('doc') already scans recursively across categories sharing the DOC id-space and recategorize already moves a doc between categories keeping its id; make it any-section, not doc-only. Migrate store.ts relocation off DocCategory/isDocCategory as part of this.
steps: Read src/artifacts/store.ts relocateArtifact and src/cli/verbs/doc.ts recategorize to see the doc-only move today.
Generalize to move-to-bucket: same-section keeps id; cross-section re-mints; migrate off DocCategory/isDocCategory.
Add tests: a same-section move keeps the id and [[id]] still resolves; a cross-section move re-mints. Run all gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0116 CAPTURE RESOLVES KIND VIA PER-VAULT TREE (PRD-0019, blockers: SLICE-0111, SLICE-0112). Fix the latent coupling in the PRD-0022 capture path (src/artifacts/capture.ts) so it rides the section/bucket tree. Today capture's resolveKind reads the bundled DEFAULT_STRUCTURE while the write step loads the per-vault Structure via loadStructure(vaultRoot) (verified: capture.ts imports both; resolveKind uses DEFAULT_STRUCTURE.kinds / DEFAULT_STRUCTURE.typeForId, fileArtifact calls loadStructure). Harmless while every vault uses the default tree, but under a custom tree capture would fail to recognize a custom bucket it is about to write into. Move kind resolution onto the SAME per-vault loaded Structure the write uses, so a custom-tree bucket is recognized and filed with a section-prefixed id via mintAndWrite. Regression + coupling fix; no direct user story.
steps: Read src/artifacts/capture.ts (resolveKind, fileArtifact, captureArtifact) to confirm resolveKind uses DEFAULT_STRUCTURE while the write uses loadStructure.
Thread the per-vault loaded Structure into kind resolution so resolveKind no longer reads DEFAULT_STRUCTURE.

Add a test: capture end-to-end against a custom-tree TEMP vault recognizes a custom bucket and files with a section-prefixed id; a re-fire is idempotent (no double-create). Never touch the real $HOME/Knowledge vault.
Run all gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0117 DELETE DEAD DOC-CATEGORY MACHINERY (PRD-0019, blockers: SLICE-0112, SLICE-0113, SLICE-0114, SLICE-0115). Capstone deletion: remove DOC_CATEGORIES, isDocCategory, defaultCategoryForDocType (and the DocCategory type) from src/artifacts/registry.ts and the `type` enum from templates/doc.md, now that every consumer routes through the section/bucket tree. Confirmed importers that MUST already be migrated by the blockers: src/artifacts/store.ts (0112 create + 0115 relocation), src/bootstrap/doctor.ts (0113), src/cli/verbs/create.ts (0112 + 0114), src/cli/verbs/doc.ts (0115). After this a doc no longer carries both a `type` field and a `category` folder that can disagree (the three-vocabularies cleanup). ADR-0028 (locked categories) is superseded by the config-declared bucket model. The build must be green at THIS commit (no dangling import).
steps: grep the tree for DOC_CATEGORIES / isDocCategory / defaultCategoryForDocType / DocCategory and confirm only registry.ts (owner) and already-migrated consumers remain.
Delete the machinery from registry.ts and the doc `type` enum from templates/doc.md.
Fix any residual reference so build + typecheck are clean; ensure a doc no longer carries a type/category that can disagree.
Note ADR-0028 superseded in progress. Add/adjust a test proving the doc-category machinery is gone and docs still create/validate. Run all gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0118 CUSTOM TREE END-TO-END + CRITERIA (PRD-0019, blockers: SLICE-0110, SLICE-0111, SLICE-0112, SLICE-0113). Prove the whole mechanism on a TEMP vault whose wiki.json declares a custom tree the tool has never seen (e.g. a `bugs` bucket and a top-level `architecture` section with its own buckets). Creating into those buckets drives folders, prefixes, and templates entirely from config, mints section-prefixed ids, and passes doctor's structural validation with zero code change. Surface each bucket's `criteria` (the agent's what-goes-where signal) through `wiki create <bucket> --help` and `wiki schema <bucket>`, both reading it from the loaded structure. Update the wiki skill doc to reflect the new `wiki create <bucket>` syntax (replacing the --category guidance). Never touch the real $HOME/Knowledge vault.
steps: Build a TEMP vault with a custom wiki.json tree (a bugs bucket + an architecture section with buckets).
Verify end-to-end on that vault: create into custom buckets drives folders/prefixes/templates from config and mints section-prefixed ids; doctor passes; nextId honors the tree.
Surface bucket criteria via `wiki create <bucket> --help` and `wiki schema <bucket>` from the loaded structure.
Update the wiki skill doc for the new create-by-bucket syntax. Run all gates green and commit.
